title: qa-dep-validation: reject phantom dep targets and self-deps
body:
# Goal

Validate that `jjf dep add <child> <target> --kind <k>`
rejects `<target>` ids that don't exist on the bookmark.
Today the verb writes the op blindly and the phantom edge
sits in the field forever.

End state: `jjf dep add A 9999999 --kind blocks` returns
a typed error and writes nothing.

# Why now

QA red-team round 2026-06-23 (see "qa-redteam" handoff)
found `jjf dep add 7e3717f 1234567 --kind blocks` succeeds
silently. The `1234567` id has never existed. The op lands
in the trailer. `7e3717f` now has a "blocks" edge to a
ghost.

Cost of leaving this unfixed:
- `jjf ready` treats the phantom blocker as "open" (since
  it's not "closed") and excludes `7e3717f` from ready
  output indefinitely.
- `jjf dep tree` shows the phantom id as an unresolvable
  child.
- Migration from asterinas will trip on stale `Blocked by:`
  references that point at issues that don't exist (renames,
  deletions in the original tree). Without the validation,
  the migration silently spreads broken edges.

# Scope

## Storage

- `Storage::add_dep_edge(child, target, kind)` does a
  resolver lookup on `target` first. If it doesn't
  resolve, return `Error::IssueNotFound { id: <target> }`.
- Same for `Storage::remove_dep_edge` (don't bother
  validating — removing a phantom is harmless / desirable).
- New: `Storage::add_dep_edge` must also reject
  `child == target` with `Error::SelfDependency { id }`.
  (See companion ticket `qa-self-dep`.)

## CLI

- `jjf dep add` surfaces the new errors as exit 2 with
  envelope kind `issue_not_found` (already exists for
  `show <bogus-id>`) and `self_dependency` (new).

## Migration concern

- The migrator (`cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate`) needs
  to handle this rejection cleanly. Two paths:
  - Resolve all deps in a second pass after all issues
    are filed (cleanest).
  - Tolerate `issue_not_found` and log per-edge for
    later manual repair (lossy but simpler).
  - Either way: the validation here forces the migrator
    to be explicit about its policy.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf dep add <real-id> <fake-id> --kind <k>` exits 2
  with `issue_not_found`.
- [ ] `jjf dep add <real-id> <real-id> --kind <k>` exits 2
  with `self_dependency`.
- [ ] `jjf new -d <fake-id>` (the inline-on-create form)
  exits 2 the same way.
- [ ] `jjf new --dep blocks:<fake-id>` exits 2 the same way.
- [ ] `jjf dep rm` against a phantom edge succeeds (removing
  a non-existent edge from the field is a no-op or removes
  the dangling entry; either is fine).
- [ ] Storage tests for both rejections.
- [ ] CLI integration tests for both error envelopes.
- [ ] `docs/cli-json.md` documents the new error kind
  `self_dependency`.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.

# Out of scope

- Hard cycle detection (parent-child A → B → A). v1
  parent-child cycle policy is "fine, cascade terminates"
  per `b6d066b agent-dep-types`. Reject self-deps only.
- Existing dangling edges (the test repo from the QA
  round has them; the production planner doesn't).
  Cleanup tooling separate.
- Type-level validation that `parent-child` edges are
  acyclic in a stricter sense. Out per the existing
  cycle policy.

# Pointers

- Implementation: `Storage::add_dep_edge` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`.
- Resolver: `Storage::resolve` in the same file.
- Error kinds today: `Error` enum in same file.
- CLI verbs: `jjf dep add` / `jjf dep rm` / `jjf new -d`
  / `jjf new --dep` in `crates/jjf/src/main.rs`.
- Repro: QA round 2026-06-23 tests 9, 10.
- Companion: `qa-self-dep` may fold into this ticket or
  stay separate; the implementation work is shared.
